title: NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard
industry: All sectors in NIS2 Annex I and II
exclusions: Does not auto-remediate controls, approve policy exceptions, or certify formal external audits; it only reflects curator-maintained CSVs and Splunk-stored evidence exports available to the search tier at run time.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.57 NIS2 Maturity Benchmark`, Dashboard Studio board `nis2-maturity-benchmark`, and nightly CSV snapshot archived to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:saved_search` with RFC 3161 sealing.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.20(1) receives contributing assurance because UC evidence proves the monitorable workflow or telemetry portion while legal approval and filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Produces NIS2 evidence for Art.20(1) using NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search for NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard, dashboard panel, and archived result export in index=audit_evidence for Art.20(1).

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(f) receives contributing assurance because UC evidence proves the monitorable workflow or telemetry portion while legal approval and filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Produces NIS2 evidence for Art.21(2)(f) using NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search for NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard, dashboard panel, and archived result export in index=audit_evidence for Art.21(2)(f).

controlTest.positiveScenario: Fixture `sample-data/uc-22.2.57-fixture.json` sets `uc_id=22.2.57-DEMO` with `status=crawl`, `assurance=high`, `last_reviewed=2024-01-15`, and omits any `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export` rows in the last 120 days so `evidence_age_days=999` and `next_best_control` recommends refreshing exports while `benchmark_rank` sorts near the top.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same obligation row but `status=run`, `last_reviewed` within 30 days, and a fresh `evidence:export` event at `_time=now()-1d` carrying `evidence_id=EVID-778899` yields `weighted_maturity≥90`, causing the sort command to leave the row outside the leadership alert threshold window (top 10) used by the alerting macro.

dataSources: `| inputlookup nis2_obligation_matrix.csv` carries `clause`, `coverage`, `assurance`, and `uc_id` rows maintained by the compliance office; `| inputlookup uc_implementation_status.csv` layers implementation telemetry `status` (crawl/walk/run), `wave`, `last_reviewed`, and `owner` for each `uc_id`; fresh attestations land in `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export` with `evidence_id`, `exported_at`, and `freshness_score` extracted at index-time. Optional normalisation uses Splunk Common Information Model Add-on ([Splunkbase 1621](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)) when multiple teams push differently named timestamp fields into the same index.
app: Splunk Enterprise 9.2+ search heads orchestrate `savedsearches.conf` schedules over CSV knowledge objects; Splunk Common Information Model Add-on ([Splunkbase 1621](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)) is optional glue for consistent field aliases (`exported_at` → `_time`) when evidence exports originate from more than one TA.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_obligation_matrix.csv
| join type=left uc_id [
    | inputlookup uc_implementation_status.csv
]
| join type=left uc_id [
    search index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(_time) as evidence_export_ts latest(evidence_id) as latest_evidence_id max(freshness_score) as freshness_score by uc_id
]
| eval evidence_age_days=if(isnotnull(evidence_export_ts), round((now()-evidence_export_ts)/86400,2), 999)
| eval review_epoch=if(isnotnull(last_reviewed) AND match(last_reviewed, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}"), strptime(last_reviewed, "%Y-%m-%d"), null())
| eval review_lag_days=if(isnotnull(review_epoch), round((now()-review_epoch)/86400,2), 999)
| eval maturity_score=case(
    status="run" AND evidence_age_days<=30 AND review_lag_days<=90, 95,
    status="walk" AND evidence_age_days<=45 AND review_lag_days<=120, 75,
    status="crawl" AND evidence_age_days<=60, 55,
    true(), 35)
| eval assurance_weight=case(lower(assurance)="high", 1.15, lower(assurance)="medium", 1.0, true(), 0.85)
| eval weighted_maturity=round(maturity_score*assurance_weight,0)
| eval next_best_control=case(
    evidence_age_days>60 AND lower(assurance)="high", "Schedule fresh evidence export + owner attestation for ".uc_id,
    weighted_maturity<50, "Advance ".uc_id." implementation wave from ".wave." with measurable testing",
    review_lag_days>180, "Force quarterly leadership review for ".clause,
    true(), "Maintain current maturity trajectory for ".owner)
| eval benchmark_rank=weighted_maturity*(100/min(100,evidence_age_days))
| sort - benchmark_rank
| table clause, coverage, assurance, uc_id, status, wave, owner, evidence_age_days, review_lag_days, maturity_score, weighted_maturity, next_best_control, latest_evidence_id, freshness_score, benchmark_rank
description: Materialises the obligation catalogue from `nis2_obligation_matrix.csv`, left-joins `uc_implementation_status.csv` for crawl/walk/run status and accountable `owner`, enriches each `uc_id` with the newest `evidence:export` payload from `index=audit_evidence`, derives `evidence_age_days` and `review_lag_days`, multiplies baseline maturity by an `assurance` weight, and ranks `next_best_control` remediation text so leadership sees which duties are slipping before external assurance interviews.
value: Executives trade static compliance spreadsheets for a single prioritized queue that aligns scarce remediation capacity with the riskiest assurance gaps—preventing duplicate projects, shortening audit prep cycles, and showing year-over-year improvement narratives to stakeholders without manually re-scoring hundreds of controls each quarter.
implementation: (1) Publish and version both CSV lookups via change control. (2) Land automated evidence exports on `index=audit_evidence`. (3) Schedule this SPL daily and push results to a Dashboard Studio JSON board for CISO staff review.
visualization: Heat strip for `weighted_maturity`, bubble chart (`benchmark_rank` vs `assurance`), sortable table with drilldown to `latest_evidence_id`, owner filter tokens sourced from `uc_implementation_status.csv`.
dataModelAcceleration: No CIM DMA required; if evidence exports are later routed through accelerated data models, add a tstats variant as `cimSpl` in a future revision.

references.title: Splunk Common Information Model Add-on on Splunkbase

knownFalsePositives: 1) **CSV publication lag after a major control uplift weekend** — engineers finished remediations but the `uc_implementation_status.csv` export still shows `crawl`. Distinguish: Git commit timestamp on the lookup bundle in the deployment repo is newer than Splunk `_reload` time. Suppress: time-bound exception row in `nis2_exception_register.csv` keyed on `uc_id` until GRC republishes. 2) **Evidence exports paused during vault maintenance** — `evidence_age_days` spikes although manual PDFs were archived offline. Distinguish: ticketing flag `evidence_pipeline=paused` on `CHG` records. Suppress: join `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change` and filter `where change_freeze=0`. 3) **Duplicate `uc_id` rows after catalogue reorganisation** two benchmarks appear for the same control family. Distinguish: `| inputlookup nis2_obligation_matrix.csv | stats dc(uc_id) by clause` >1. Suppress: deduplicate with `nis2_uc_duplicate_map.csv` lookup keeping canonical `uc_id`. 4) **Owner field lists a distribution list alias** distorting accountability narratives. Distinguish: `owner` contains `DL-` prefix or `@` mailing list domains. Suppress: lookup `nis2_owner_alias.csv` mapping lists to named individuals for reporting only. 5) **Assurance label typo (`High` vs `high`)** reduces weighting unpredictably. Distinguish: rogue casing in CSV. Suppress: normalize with `| eval assurance=lower(assurance)` in a macro or enforce validation during CSV ingest via Git pre-commit hook.
equipmentModels: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (REST API for saved search inventory, _internal for scheduler health)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, correlation search and content management audit)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Indexes**: `audit_evidence` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `nis2_obligation_matrix.csv`, `uc_implementation_status.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_obligation_matrix.csv','uc_implementation_status.csv')`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.20(1)`. The control objective is: "Produces NIS2 evidence for Art.20(1) using NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC — no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into `audit_evidence` under `evidence:export`, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


Three operational considerations, authored alongside this UC, apply:


• **Hint 1:** Publish and version both CSV lookups via change control. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 2:** Land automated evidence exports on `index=audit_evidence`. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

• **Hint 3:** Schedule this SPL daily and push results to a Dashboard Studio JSON board for CISO staff review. Schedule cadence is a tradeoff between regulatory latency and search-head load; document the chosen cadence and the reasoning in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard," the aggregation produces the result shape required by the NIS2 control — each row is a summary record the regulation expects to be presentable at audit time.

This search correlates the outer dataset (index=`audit_evidence`, sourcetype=`evidence:export`) with a subsearch on `uc_id` across the last year. `type=left` preserves all rows from the outer search; nulls in the inner side indicate missing data that may itself constitute a finding.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

Time-zone sensitivity: the `strptime(last_reviewed, "%Y-%m-%d")` conversion assumes the source timestamp is in the Splunk server's local time zone unless the format includes `%z`. If `last_reviewed` is emitted in UTC by the source system but Splunk's search head runs in a non-UTC zone, the resulting epoch will be off by the zone offset and the subsequent `where _time > last_reviewed_epoch` comparison will produce systematic false positives or negatives. Always validate by comparing `strftime(last_reviewed_epoch, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")` against a known-good reference event before promoting to production.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.57: NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -365d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.57,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `clause`, `coverage`, `assurance`, `uc_id`, `status`, `wave`, `owner`, `evidence_age_days`, `review_lag_days`, `maturity_score`. Run:

```spl
index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table clause, coverage, assurance, uc_id, status, wave, owner, evidence_age_days
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(d) Threshold calibration:** Before enabling as a production alert, run the base search with the threshold removed and profile the distribution of `evidence_age_days`:

```spl
<base search> earliest=-30d
| stats perc50(evidence_age_days) as p50 perc90(evidence_age_days) as p90 perc99(evidence_age_days) as p99 max(evidence_age_days) as hi
```

The current threshold `30` should sit between p90 and p99 for a balanced signal-to-noise ratio. If it's below p50, expect heavy alert volume; if above p99, expect under-detection.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.57: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Heat Strip For `Weighted Maturity` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Bubble Chart (``benchmark_rank` vs `assurance``):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Sortable Table With Drilldown To `Latest Evidence Id` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 4 — Owner Filter Tokens Sourced From `Uc Implementation Status.Csv` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: We turn a long list of cyber duties into a simple priority list—who owns each item, how current the proof is, and what to improve next—so leaders spend their limited time on what actually moves the needle.
